title: Does China’s latest AI model finally equal US rivals? What scientists think
excerpt: ‘Kimi K3’ can match or outperform cutting-edge models, says Moonshot AI. But its large size might limit uptake.
content_html:
The latest Chinese-built large language model (LLM) is impressing scientists with its size and capabilities. Last week, Beijing-based Moonshot AI unveiled ‘Kimi K3’, a powerful reasoning LLM that can handle large swathes of text. The company’s own tests found that K3 can match or outperform rival US models on tasks such as coding and manipulating spreadsheets.

Not since DeepSeek has a Chinese artificial-intelligence model caused quite so much buzz. “It is a turning point,” says Joel Pearson, a cognitive neuroscientist at UNSW Sydney in Australia, who studies how AI is affecting people’s lives. “People are calling it a ‘Sputnik moment’,” he adds.

Three days after the model’s release, Moonshot AI said it had paused new sign-ups to K3 because demand had pushed its system close to the limits of its processing capacity.

The model’s launch, on 16 July, came just before the 2026 Artificial Intelligence World Conference in Shanghai. The conference began with Chinese President Xi Jinping announcing the formal start of a global alliance to create regulation that ensures AI is safe and benefits people. “In China’s view, all countries should take a people-centred approach and develop AI for the positive and for good,” Xi said at the conference, adding that AI should be a driver for “shared prosperity and common security”.

The timing of Kimi K3’s release is significant, says Mehwish Nasim, an AI researcher at the University of Western Australia in Perth. The model was launched just weeks after Claude Fable 5 by US company Anthropic, and days after OpenAI released its latest GPT-5.6 model. “China is signalling its ambition not only to build frontier AI systems but also to help shape the international AI ecosystem and its governance,” she says.

Largest open-weight model

K3 is open weight, like its predecessor Kimi K2 and DeepSeek models, which were developed by a Hangzhou-based firm of the same name, meaning its core components are publicly available and can be downloaded and modified by researchers for free, while information on the model’s training is private. It can also be accessed using an ‘application programming interface’, which allows software to communicate, at a lower cost than proprietary LLMs, such as those owned by OpenAI, Google and Anthropic. Those LLMs are closed weight, meaning they cannot be downloaded and modified.

Moonshot says that the weights, or parameters, will be released on 27 July. Rahul Shome, a robotics and AI researcher at the Australian National University in Canberra, says that there has been a push from consumers, developers and researchers to make AI models open weight. K3 is particularly exciting because it would be the largest open-weight model, says Shome, with 2.8 trillion parameters and a working memory of one million tokens (the units of text used by AI models). The model is too large to run on personal devices, however, so it will probably require significant investment by institutions to run it, he adds.

K3’s large working memory means it could remember the contents of thousands of lines of code or a whole book and reduce the likelihood of producing fabricated information called hallucination, says Niusha Shafiabady, a computational-intelligence researcher at Australian Catholic University in Sydney. Shafiabady says that she is going to test K3 for herself by asking it to summarize research findings.

Closing the gap

The performance and capabilities of open-weight models have historically lagged behind those of US proprietary ones. But the gap seems to be the tightest it has ever been, says Aaron Snoswell, an AI accountability researcher at the Queensland University of Technology Generative AI Lab, Brisbane.

Pearson says that Chinese open-weight models are changing how investors and users perceive the value of US frontier models. Google, Anthropic and OpenAI spend billions of dollars training their models compared with the much lower budgets of Chinese companies. Moreover, users could lose access to US models if the government decides to impose restrictions, says Pearson. Last month, Anthropic temporarily disabled its most advanced models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after it was directed by the US government to suspend access for foreign nationals. Access to Fable 5 was restored this month, but Mythos 5 is only available to specific US organizations.

Toby Walsh, a computer scientist at UNSW, says that Chinese AI companies have been able to build frontier AI models despite the United States restricting China’s access to advanced AI chips, citing national-security concerns. “One suspects that necessity here was the mother of invention,” he adds.

sentences.zh: 堪培拉澳大利亚国立大学的机器人和人工智能研究员拉胡尔·肖姆 (Rahul Shome) 表示，消费者、开发者和研究人员一直在推动人工智能模型开放重量。

sentences.en: K3 is particularly exciting because it would be the largest open-weight model, says Shome, with 2.8 trillion parameters and a working memory of one million tokens (the units of text used by AI models).
sentences.zh: Shome 表示，K3 特别令人兴奋，因为它将成为最大的开放权重模型，拥有 2.8 万亿个参数和 100 万个标记（AI 模型使用的文本单位）的工作记忆。

sentences.en: The model is too large to run on personal devices, however, so it will probably require significant investment by institutions to run it, he adds.
sentences.zh: 然而，该模型太大，无法在个人设备上运行，因此可能需要机构进行大量投资才能运行它，他补充道。

Shafiabady says that she is going to test K3 for herself by asking it to summarize research findings.

sentences.en: K3’s large working memory means it could remember the contents of thousands of lines of code or a whole book and reduce the likelihood of producing fabricated information called hallucination, says Niusha Shafiabady, a computational-intelligence researcher at Australian Catholic University in Sydney.
sentences.zh: 悉尼澳大利亚天主教大学计算智能研究员 Niusha Shafiabady 表示，K3 的大工作记忆意味着它可以记住数千行代码或整本书的内容，并减少产生称为幻觉的捏造信息的可能性。

sentences.en: Shafiabady says that she is going to test K3 for herself by asking it to summarize research findings.
sentences.zh: Shafiabady 表示，她将通过要求 K3 总结研究结果来亲自测试 K3。

sentences.en: Closing the gap
sentences.zh: 缩小差距

blocks.html_en: The performance and capabilities of open-weight models have historically lagged behind those of US proprietary ones. But the gap seems to be the tightest it has ever been, says Aaron Snoswell, an AI accountability researcher at the Queensland University of Technology Generative AI Lab, Brisbane.

sentences.en: The performance and capabilities of open-weight models have historically lagged behind those of US proprietary ones.
sentences.zh: 开放权重模型的性能和功能历来落后于美国专有模型。

sentences.en: But the gap seems to be the tightest it has ever been, says Aaron Snoswell, an AI accountability researcher at the Queensland University of Technology Generative AI Lab, Brisbane.
sentences.zh: 但布里斯班昆士兰科技大学人工智能生成实验室的人工智能问责研究员亚伦·斯诺斯韦尔 (Aaron Snoswell) 表示，这种差距似乎是有史以来最窄的。

blocks.html_en: Pearson says that Chinese open-weight models are changing how investors and users perceive the value of US frontier models. Google, Anthropic and OpenAI spend billions of dollars training their models compared with the much lower budgets of Chinese companies. Moreover, users could lose access to US models if the government decides to impose restrictions, says Pearson. Last month, Anthropic temporarily disabled its most advanced models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after it was directed by the US government to suspend access for foreign nationals. Access to Fable 5 was restored this month, but Mythos 5 is only available to specific US organizations.

sentences.en: Pearson says that Chinese open-weight models are changing how investors and users perceive the value of US frontier models.
sentences.zh: 皮尔逊表示，中国的开放权重模型正在改变投资者和用户对美国前沿模型价值的看法。

sentences.en: Google, Anthropic and OpenAI spend billions of dollars training their models compared with the much lower budgets of Chinese companies.
sentences.zh: 谷歌、Anthropic 和 OpenAI 花费数十亿美元训练他们的模型，而中国公司的预算要低得多。

sentences.en: Moreover, users could lose access to US models if the government decides to impose restrictions, says Pearson.
sentences.zh: 此外，皮尔逊表示，如果政府决定施加限制，用户可能会无法使用美国模型。

sentences.en: Last month, Anthropic temporarily disabled its most advanced models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after it was directed by the US government to suspend access for foreign nationals.
sentences.zh: 上个月，在美国政府指示暂停外国人访问后，Anthropic 暂时对所有用户禁用了其最先进的游戏《神鬼寓言 5》和《神话 5》。

sentences.en: Access to Fable 5 was restored this month, but Mythos 5 is only available to specific US organizations.
sentences.zh: 《神鬼寓言 5》的访问权限已于本月恢复，但《神话 5》仅适用于特定的美国组织。

blocks.html_en: Toby Walsh, a computer scientist at UNSW, says that Chinese AI companies have been able to build frontier AI models despite the United States restricting China’s access to advanced AI chips, citing national-security concerns. “One suspects that necessity here was the mother of invention,” he adds.

sentences.en: Toby Walsh, a computer scientist at UNSW, says that Chinese AI companies have been able to build frontier AI models despite the United States restricting China’s access to advanced AI chips, citing national-security concerns.
sentences.zh: 新南威尔士大学计算机科学家托比·沃尔什表示，尽管美国以国家安全为由限制中国获得先进人工智能芯片，但中国人工智能公司仍然能够构建前沿人工智能模型。

sentences.en: “One suspects that necessity here was the mother of invention,” he adds.
sentences.zh: “人们怀疑这里的需要是发明之母，”他补充道。

content_html_zh:
中国最新构建的大语言模型（LLM）以其规模和能力给科学家留下了深刻的印象。 上周，总部位于北京的 Moonshot AI 推出了“Kimi K3”，这是一个强大的推理法学硕士，可以处理大量文本。 该公司自己的测试发现，K3 在编码和操作电子表格等任务上可以与美国竞争对手的模型相媲美或超越。

自从 DeepSeek 以来，中国的人工智能模型还没有引起如此大的关注。 “这是一个转折点，”澳大利亚悉尼新南威尔士大学的认知神经科学家乔尔·皮尔森（Joel Pearson）说，他研究人工智能如何影响人们的生活。 “人们称之为‘人造卫星时刻’，”他补充道。

该模型发布三天后，Moonshot AI 表示已暂停 K3 的新注册，因为需求已使其系统接近处理能力的极限。

该模型于 7 月 16 日发布，恰逢 2026 年上海人工智能世界大会之前。 会议开始时，中国国家主席习近平宣布正式启动全球联盟，以制定监管措施，确保人工智能安全并造福人类。 习近平在会上表示：“中国认为，各国都应该坚持以人民为中心，积极向善地发展人工智能。”他补充说，人工智能应该成为“共同繁荣、共同安全”的驱动力。

珀斯西澳大利亚大学人工智能研究员 Mehwish Nasim 表示，Kimi K3 的发布时机意义重大。 该模型是在美国公司 Anthropic 推出 Claude Fable 5 几周后推出的，而几天前 OpenAI 发布了最新的 GPT-5.6 模型。 她表示：“中国正在释放其雄心，不仅要建立前沿人工智能系统，还要帮助塑造国际人工智能生态系统及其治理。”

最大的开放式重量模型

K3 与它的前身 Kimi K2 和 DeepSeek 模型一样，都是开放权重的，这两个模型都是由杭州的同名公司开发的，这意味着它的核心组件是公开的，研究人员可以免费下载和修改，而模型训练的信息是私有的。 它还可以使用“应用程序编程接口”进行访问，该接口允许软件以比专有法学硕士（例如 OpenAI、谷歌和 Anthropic 拥有的那些）更低的成本进行通信。 这些法学硕士是封闭式的，这意味着它们无法下载和修改。

Moonshot 表示，权重或参数将于 7 月 27 日发布。 堪培拉澳大利亚国立大学的机器人和人工智能研究员拉胡尔·肖姆 (Rahul Shome) 表示，消费者、开发者和研究人员一直在推动人工智能模型开放重量。 Shome 表示，K3 特别令人兴奋，因为它将成为最大的开放权重模型，拥有 2.8 万亿个参数和 100 万个标记（AI 模型使用的文本单位）的工作记忆。 然而，该模型太大，无法在个人设备上运行，因此可能需要机构进行大量投资才能运行它，他补充道。

悉尼澳大利亚天主教大学计算智能研究员 Niusha Shafiabady 表示，K3 的大工作记忆意味着它可以记住数千行代码或整本书的内容，并减少产生称为幻觉的捏造信息的可能性。 Shafiabady 表示，她将通过要求 K3 总结研究结果来亲自测试 K3。

缩小差距

开放权重模型的性能和功能历来落后于美国专有模型。 但布里斯班昆士兰科技大学人工智能生成实验室的人工智能问责研究员亚伦·斯诺斯韦尔 (Aaron Snoswell) 表示，这种差距似乎是有史以来最窄的。

皮尔逊表示，中国的开放权重模型正在改变投资者和用户对美国前沿模型价值的看法。 谷歌、Anthropic 和 OpenAI 花费数十亿美元训练他们的模型，而中国公司的预算要低得多。 此外，皮尔逊表示，如果政府决定施加限制，用户可能会无法使用美国模型。 上个月，在美国政府指示暂停外国人访问后，Anthropic 暂时对所有用户禁用了其最先进的游戏《神鬼寓言 5》和《神话 5》。 《神鬼寓言 5》的访问权限已于本月恢复，但《神话 5》仅适用于特定的美国组织。

新南威尔士大学计算机科学家托比·沃尔什表示，尽管美国以国家安全为由限制中国获得先进人工智能芯片，但中国人工智能公司仍然能够构建前沿人工智能模型。 “人们怀疑这里的需要是发明之母，”他补充道。
